description: v1 sanity: 4 LME prices + 1d returns, 100 trees
notes: Pipeline green. Per-fold runtime ~560s for 424 GPU XGB fits — GPU warmup dominates. Minimal features already meaningful (well above zero).

description: v2 full features (143 assets × 6 feats + cal), 500 trees depth 6 defaults
notes: Heavy overfitting: overall Sharpe dropped from v1 0.157 to 0.070 despite much richer features. Fold 0 negative. Default-ish XGBoost hparams too aggressive for ~1500 rows/target. Need heavier reg + fewer trees.

description: v3 regularized: depth 4, mcw 20, colsample 0.5, gamma 0.1, 300 trees
notes: Slight improvement over v2 but still bad vs v1 (0.157). Fold 1 negative. Reg helped compute time (walltime down to 206s from 940s) but only marginal score gain. Feature set (861) may be root cause more than hparams.
WORSE than v2. Per-target pred z-scoring hurts: promoted noisy predictions, suppressed signal ones. Dont use pred-std normalization.
Identical to v2 (deterministic threading). Threading confirmed ~25% faster wall-clock.

description: v6 v2 + per-target spread features (log A/B, dev, z20)
notes: Spread features improved v2 baseline 0.070 -> 0.096. Still below v1 0.157. v2-feature noise still dominates. Spread features ARE useful but need cleaner base.

description: v7 heavy reg 100 trees depth 4 mcw 20 cols 0.3 lambda 5
notes: Heavy reg with v2 features didnt help. Same as v2 baseline. Reg alone insufficient when features are noisy.

description: v8 v1-hparams 100 trees depth 4 lr 05 mcw 5 + v2 features
notes: v1 hparams + v2 features. Overall 0.10 vs v1 0.157. Fold 0 negative — features add noise that hurts on regime shift. Fold 3 BEAT v1 (0.218 vs 0.151) — features help when data is in-distribution. Conclusion: v2 features have weak signal but high noise across folds.

params.features: ~25 per target (A,B asset feats + spread + 3 market proxies + cal)

notes: Per-target focused feats. Better fold consistency than v2/v8 (no negative folds). But fold 3 (0.093) weaker than v1 (0.151), so overall (0.110) below v1 (0.157). Per-target focus too restrictive — losing useful cross-asset signal.

description: v10 v9 + raw log-price + more horizons (~46 feats)
notes: WORSE than v9 (0.110). Adding more features (raw prices + more horizons) hurt — overfitting from extra features dominates the gain. Simpler v9 better.

description: v11 v1 hparams + 4 LME 5d & 20d ret (16 feats)
notes: Slight expansion of v1 (8 -> 16 LME feats). Worse than v1 (0.157). Adding 5d/20d returns hurts. v1s 8 features are surprisingly optimal.

description: v12 v8 hparams (v1-style) + v6 spread feats
notes: v8 + spread feats. Better than v8 (0.10). Fold 3 best yet (0.213). Still below v1 due to fold 0 negative.

description: v13 v1 LME + per-target A/B asset + spread feats

params.features: v1 LME (8) + cal (3) + per-target A B (4 each) + spread (3) = ~22

notes: BEAT v1 (0.157)! All folds positive, fold 0 jumped to 0.167 (vs v1 0.102), fold 2 0.245 (vs v1 0.174). Hybrid approach works.

description: v14 v1 LME features + per-target spread feats only

params.features: v1 LME (8) + per-target spread (3) = 11 (or 8 for single targets)

notes: NEW BEST. +0.035 over v1. Just 3 spread feats added per spread target. Beats v13 (which added A/B asset feats) — minimal addition is best.

params.features: 8 LME + 3 cal + per-target [spread, 3 windows × (dev, z) = 7 spread feats] = 18 (or 11 single)

notes: NEW BEST. +0.046 over v1, +0.010 over v14. Multi-window spread (5/20/60d) and cal features helped. Folds 0 (0.268) and 2 (0.259) jumped.

description: v16 v14 features + hparams: 200 trees depth 5 lr 0.03 mcw 10
notes: Slight regression from v14 (0.192). v14s 100 trees depth 4 hparams are well-tuned. Going deeper/longer doesnt help.

description: v17 v14 features but spread window=5d (short-term reversion)
notes: Same as v14 (0.192) almost exactly. 5d vs 20d window similar. v15 wins by combining multiple windows.

params.features: v15 + h-day and 2h-day spread return + spread vol20 per target (extra 3)

notes: Close to v15 (0.202). Horizon-matched features did not add value over the existing multi-window spread features.
Bagging 3 seeds of v15 did not help; slightly worse than single v15 (0.202). XGBoost is already stable on this data.

description: v20 v15 + JPX Gold/Platinum prices & 1d returns in common tail

params.features: v15 + 4 JPX closes + 4 JPX 1d ret (common tail 19 instead of 11)

notes: Worse than v15 (0.202). Adding JPX common features hurt fold 3 heavily. Extra noise dominates.
Slight regression from v15 (0.202). Stochastic tree sampling did not help.
NEW BEST. Ensemble v14+v15 beat v15 alone (0.202). All folds >0.14, fold 0 great at 0.24.
Huber loss did not help. MSE better for this data. But fold 2 hit 0.273, highest single fold.

description: v24 v15 with spread windows 3/10/40 (different from 5/20/60)
notes: 3/10/40 windows worse than 5/20/60. v15 window choice is good.
3-way ensemble equal-weight. Slightly worse than v22 (0.2103). Adding v17 dilutes v14+v15. 2-way ensemble is enough.

params.features: v14 + 2x v15 + v17 weighted average, 3 models per target

notes: NEW BEST. Weighted ensemble beat v22 (0.2103) and v25 equal (0.2083) by small margin. Matches diagnostic prediction.

description: v27 v15 + LME vol20 features (4 regime indicators)
notes: Worse than v15 (0.202). LME vol features hurt fold 2 especially. Dont add vol to common tail.

description: v28 v15 + minimal JPX (Gold+Platinum Std only +raw +ret1 +vol20)

params.features: v15 + 6 JPX feats (2 raw, 2 ret1, 2 vol20) in common tail

notes: Very slight gain over v15 (0.202). Minimal JPX helps a bit but not enough to beat ensembles v22/v26.
NEW BEST. +0.005 over v26 (0.2110). Adding v28 (with JPX features) to ensemble adds diversity and boosts overall Sharpe.

params.features: 3 models (v15 multi-window, v17 5d-window, v28 multi-window+JPX), equal weights

notes: NEW BEST. +0.008 over v29. Matches diagnostic perfectly. 3-model diverse ensemble: v15 (multi-window), v17 (short window), v28 (JPX + multi-window).
NEW BEST. Slight improvement over v30 (0.2181). Weighted with v28 (JPX) dominant, v17 secondary, v15 base.

params.features: 4 models weighted, includes v1 for diversity

notes: NEW BEST. 4-way ensemble matches diagnostic. Adding v1 small weight helps fold 3 robustness. Improvement over v1 baseline: +0.062 (39% relative).

params.features: v15 + 6 FX feats (2 raw + 2 ret1 + 2 vol20)

notes: HURT badly vs v15 (0.202). Fold 3 collapsed to 0.071. FX features add noise even in minimal form.

description: v34 v15 + minimal per-target A/B (ret5 + vol20 only)

params.features: v15 + 4 per-target A/B asset feats (A_ret5, A_vol20, B_ret5, B_vol20)

notes: Slightly hurt vs v15 (0.202). Even 4 per-target asset feats is too many; v13s 8 was worse (0.178). v15s pair spread already captures enough about A vs B.

description: v35 v32 ensemble + K=200 selection trick (in-sample ranking)
notes: K-trick did NOT help with in-sample ranking (basically same as v32 0.2191). Need honest OOF ranking — in-sample prefers targets where ensemble overfits, not where it generalizes. Next: nested CV inside fit().

description: v36 v32 ensemble + K=200 with nested 3-fold internal CV selection
notes: HURT vs v32. Internal CV rankings too noisy to transfer. Per-target corr Spearman across folds = 0.15-0.22. Data-driven K selection fundamentally fragile on this data.

description: v37 v32 ensemble + structural K (lag>=3 only, 212 targets)
notes: NEW BEST. +0.006 over v32. Structural K (lag>=3) is reliable, no data-dependent selection. Per-fold cross-fold ranking rho only 0.15-0.22, so observed-corr K selection is too noisy to transfer (v35 and v36 failed).

description: ensemble of 4 diverse md=3 tuned configs (trials 350/289/371/172)
notes: tiny +0.0003 over best single 0.2348; fold 3 delta -0.106 — all 4 components are md=3 shallow+many-trees, correlated errors on fold 3. Consider adding md=8 (trial 14) for anti-correlation.

description: ensemble k=5: md=3 top (350,289,371) + md=4 fold-3-balanced (61) + md=8 pre-BO winner (14)
notes: +0.0025 over v40 (0.2351). Fold 3 up 0.158->0.171. Anti-correlated picks (md=4 trial 61 with high f3 + md=8 trial 14) paid off. last-fold-delta -0.091 vs v40 -0.106.

description: v42 rank-y + trial 350 hparams (md=3 BO leader)
notes: rank-y hurts raw-y-tuned hparams by 0.06. last-fold-delta -0.104.

description: v42 rank-y + trial 14 hparams (md=8 session-1 leader)
notes: rank-y hurts. Fold 3 improved slightly vs raw. delta -0.064.

description: v42 rank-y + trial 61 hparams (md=4 fold-3 balanced)
notes: rank-y overall 0.160 but fold 3 = 0.184 with POSITIVE last-fold-delta +0.033 — uniquely regularized fold performance. Possibly useful ensemble component.

description: v43: multi-quantile XGB (9 alphas 0.1..0.9) on trial-350 base hparams, rank targets by predicted 0.5-quantile. K-trick lag>=4 & has_LME (71 targets). v38_tune 31-feature pool.
notes: 3 way quantile-regression comparison: median (this) vs mean vs expected-rank. Weakest of the 3. last-fold-delta -0.0479. Runtime ~52 min/fold (~15x slower than MSE trial 350) due to multi-output quantile trees.

description: v43: multi-quantile XGB, rank targets by mean of 9 quantile predictions.
notes: Mean beats median by +0.006. Predictive marginals appear skewed enough that mean carries more info than the 0.5-quantile alone. last-fold-delta -0.0666.

description: v43: multi-quantile XGB, rank targets by expected rank (per date: global-rank all 71x9 quantile samples, score=row mean of target's 9 sample ranks). Rearrangement-inequality-optimal score under empirical-CDF marginals.
notes: Best of the 3 quantile variants. +0.0026 over mean, +0.0083 over median. The heteroscedasticity-aware ranking captures a small but positive signal. Still -0.025 below v41 ensemble (0.2376); trial-350 hparams were tuned for MSE loss and may not be optimal for quantile. last-fold-delta -0.0674.
